description: Assertion edge cases: .textContent direct access (still user-visible because screen query is in the chain), .not.toThrow() on render (user-visible: component renders without crashing), and toHaveLength on a variable from screen.getAllByRole (user-visible but interpreter cannot trace variable back to screen query). Tests interpreter boundary between user-visible and implementation-detail assertions.

expectedClassifications.notes: screen.getByRole().textContent + toBe -- screen query in expect arg, user-visible
.not.toThrow() on render -- rendering without crash IS user-visible. Interpreter emits ASSERTION_IMPLEMENTATION because toThrow is not in userVisibleMatchers. Known interpreter gap.
toHaveLength on variable from screen.getAllByRole -- user-visible (DOM element count), but interpreter cannot trace variable assignment back to screen query. Known interpreter gap.
no local afterEach -- global cleanup handles it
